# 2026 FIFA World Cup — Group Stage Match Results (June 11–16, 2026)

## Overview

The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicked off on **June 11, 2026**, co-hosted by the **United States, Canada, and Mexico**. This is the first 48-team World Cup in history, featuring 12 groups of 4 teams, with 104 total matches. The group stage runs from June 11 to approximately June 28, 2026, with the final at MetLife Stadium, New Jersey on July 19.

**Important caveat:** My search tools (web search, web fetch) were unavailable during this research session. All data below was extracted from locally cached research files, forecast traces, and web search result snippets indexed by a forecasting system as of June 15–16, 2026. **I was only able to confirm specific match scores for 3 matches.** The tournament had many more matches during this window that I could not retrieve results for.

---

## Confirmed Match Results (June 11–16, 2026)

### Match 1: Opening Match — Group A
| Date | Match | Score | Venue | Group | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| **June 11, 2026** | **Mexico vs South Africa** | **2–0** | Mexico City (Estadio Azteca) | A | Opening match of the tournament. Described as a "chaotic match" with **three red cards** shown. Ended "decades of host-nation curse" (referring to past opening match struggles by host nations). |

**Source:** defirate.com (2026 World Cup Odds Tracker), scraped June 15, 2026: *"Mexico won the opener 2-0 over South Africa, ending decades of host-nation curse in a chaotic match that saw three red cards shown."*

---

### Match (Group D, Matchday 1)
| Date | Match | Score | Venue | Group | Goal Scorers |
|------|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------------|
| **June 12, 2026** | **USA vs Paraguay** | **4–1** | SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, CA | D | **Folarin Balogun (×2)**, Bobadilla (OG), **Giovanni Reyna** |

**Key details:**
- USA scored in the **7th minute** of the match
- Balogun scored both goals in the **first half**
- Christian Pulisic was also heavily involved (celebration noted by Reuters)
- This was the highest-scoring opening match for the USMNT in World Cup history
- Manager: **Mauricio Pochettino**
- **Confirmed by:** ESPN ("USA 4-1 Paraguay (Jun 12, 2026) Final Score"), NBC News, CBS Sports, FOX Sports
- Note: A Polymarket snippet erroneously cited a "4-3" scoreline, but this was debunked by multiple authoritative sources confirming **4–1**.

---

### Match (Group D, Matchday 1)
| Date | Match | Score | Venue | Group | Goal Scorers |
|------|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------------|
| **June 13 or 14, 2026** | **Australia vs Turkey** | **2–0** | BC Place, Vancouver | D | **Irankunda**, **Metcalfe** |

**Key details:**
- Turkey (Türkiye) failed to score in their first World Cup match since 2002
- This was Turkey's first World Cup appearance since their 3rd-place finish in 2002
- Australia's Socceroos dominated the match
- **Confirmed by:** bolavip.com ("Australia vs Turkiye ended in a 2-0 victory for the Socceroos"), FIFA standings data, olympics.com

---

## Group D Standings After Matchday 1

| Pos | Team | P | W | D | L | GF | GA | GD | Pts |
|-----|------|---|---|---|---|----|----|-----|-----|
| 1 | **United States** | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 4 | 1 | +3 | **3** |
| 2 | **Australia** | 1 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 2 | 0 | +2 | **3** |
| 3 | **Turkey** | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 2 | −2 | **0** |
| 4 | **Paraguay** | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 4 | −3 | **0** |

**Source:** FIFA.com via ESPN API, confirmed June 15, 2026.

---

## Tournament-Wide Context (as of June 15–16, 2026)

- **5 days** of group stage matches completed (June 11–15)
- **World Cup winner favorites** (Kalshi/Polymarket, as of ~June 15):
  - Spain: +514 (16.3% implied)
  - France: +521 (16.1% implied)
  - Portugal: +818 (10.9% implied)
- **Group A:** Mexico, South Korea, Czechia, South Africa
  - Mexico 2–0 South Africa confirmed (June 11)
- **Group D:** USA, Paraguay, Australia, Turkey
  - USA 4–1 Paraguay (June 12)
  - Australia 2–0 Turkey (June 13/14)
  - Matchday 2: USA vs Australia, Turkey vs Paraguay — scheduled for **June 19**

---

## What Could NOT Be Confirmed

Due to the unavailability of web search/fetch tools, I was **unable to retrieve** match results for **any groups other than A (partially) and D** during June 11–16. The tournament would have had matches across many of the 12 groups during this period. Specifically missing:
- Groups B, C, E, F, G, H, I, J, K, L match results
- Full Group A results beyond the opening match
- Detailed minute-by-minute data for confirmed matches
- Attendance figures
- VAR incidents (beyond the 3 red cards in Mexico vs South Africa)

key_facts: The 2026 FIFA World Cup opened on June 11, 2026, with Mexico defeating South Africa 2-0 in Mexico City in a chaotic match featuring three red cards.
USA defeated Paraguay 4-1 on June 12, 2026 at SoFi Stadium (Group D Matchday 1). Folarin Balogun scored twice (first half brace), with additional goals from a Bobadilla own goal and Giovanni Reyna.
Australia defeated Turkey (Türkiye) 2-0 on June 13/14, 2026 at BC Place, Vancouver (Group D Matchday 1). Goals by Irankunda and Metcalfe.
Group D standings after Matchday 1: USA 3pts (GD +3), Australia 3pts (GD +2), Turkey 0pts (GD -2), Paraguay 0pts (GD -3).
This is the first 48-team FIFA World Cup, with 12 groups of 4 teams, 104 total matches, hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico (June 11 – July 19, 2026).
USA's 4-1 win was described as the highest-ever single World Cup match goal tally for the USMNT.
Turkey's loss to Australia marked their first World Cup match since 2002 (when they finished 3rd place).
USA manager is Mauricio Pochettino. USA's Folarin Balogun is the early tournament top scorer in Group D with 2 goals.
World Cup winner favorites as of June 15, 2026: Spain +514 (16.3% implied), France +521 (16.1% implied), Portugal +818 (10.9% implied) per Kalshi/Polymarket.
Mexico's 2-0 opening match win ended a noted 'host-nation curse' referring to past opening match struggles by host nations.
Group D Matchday 2 (USA vs Australia, Turkey vs Paraguay) was scheduled for June 19, 2026.
Only 3 match results could be confirmed (Mexico 2-0 South Africa, USA 4-1 Paraguay, Australia 2-0 Turkey) due to web tool unavailability; many more matches were played June 11-16 across 12 groups.
